name: github-sarif-preflight
description: Check SARIF against GitHub Code Scanning's consumer profile and the current checkout before upload.
targetUsers: DevSecOps engineers and scanner maintainers uploading third-party SARIF to GitHub Code Scanning.
jobToBeDone: Check SARIF against GitHub's documented consumer profile and the current repository checkout before upload, then identify processing failures or broken source links in time to fix scanner output.
distributionPath: Checksum-verified reproducible Linux and macOS archives, go install, and a source-built offline composite GitHub Action.
successMetric: Within 30 days, one unrelated external repository uses the preflight to avoid a SARIF processing failure or repair an alert source link to a real checkout path.

demandEvidence.independenceKey: android lint uri base processing failure

alternatives.name: GitHub SARIF support documentation
alternatives.gap: Documents the consumer subset but does not inspect a SARIF file against the current checkout.

alternatives.name: github/codeql-action upload-sarif preprocessing
alternatives.gap: Preprocesses upload inputs but does not expose one credential-free local consumer-profile and checkout preflight.

alternatives.name: Microsoft Sarif.Multitool 5.5.0
alternatives.gap: Accepted all four controlled consumer-profile failure fixtures without a generic validation error.

alternatives.name: jq JSON shape inspection
alternatives.gap: Checks structural predicates but does not resolve GitHub profile rules and repository paths together.

duplicateSearch.summary: Compared GitHub documentation and upload preprocessing, Sarif.Multitool 5.5.0, and jq against four controlled fixtures; none combined consumer-profile checks with the current checkout.

differentiation: An offline read-only preflight limited to GitHub's documented SARIF consumer profile and POSIX checkout paths, with deterministic diagnostics and explicit unknowns instead of a general SARIF validator.
